1767: that might be available to Mac-Gray, including a potential sale of the company. The Board also discussed, with input from the members of the Transaction Committee, whether it would be appropriate for
1768: each of Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg to be excluded from the Board's consideration of strategic alternatives in view of the possibility that Mr. MacDonald and/or Moab could be
1769: asked to partner with a potential acquiror given their respective equity stakes in Mac-Gray. Following this discussion, the Board decided, and Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg agreed,
1770: that it would be appropriate to exclude Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg from the Board's evaluation of strategic alternatives and any negotiations with third parties that might take
1771: place. Mr. MacDonald excused himself after such discussion. In furtherance of the conclusion to exclude Mr. MacDonald and Mr. Rothenberg from the Board's evaluation of strategic
1772: alternatives, the Board then established the Special Committee, comprised of the six independent and disinterested members of the Board (namely, Mr. Bullock, Mr. Daoust,
1773: Mr. Hyman, William F. Meagher, Bruce A. Percelay and Alastair G. Robinson), to, among other things, explore strategic alternatives to enhance value to Mac-Gray's stockholders and to recommend
1774: to the Board the advisability of any such strategic alternative. In connection with this delegation of authority, the Special Committee was further authorized to (a) consider and evaluate all
1775: proposals that might be received by Mac-Gray in connection with a possible sale or other significant business transaction, through any form of transaction, including, without limitation, merger, stock
1776: purchase, asset purchase, recapitalization, reorganization, going-private transaction, consolidation, amalgamation or other transaction, (b) participate in and direct the negotiation of the
1777: material terms and conditions of any such transaction, (c) consider any alternatives to any such transaction, including without limitation, Mac-Gray's continuing to operate as an independent
1778: company and (d) recommend to the Board the advisability of entering into a definitive agreement with respect to any such transaction. Mr. Bullock was appointed chairman of the Special
1779: Committee. It was also determined that the Transaction Committee would function as a sub-committee of the Special Committee for the purpose of taking any administrative actions to discharge the duties
